25302c0c00462c6d: [{"severity": "HIGH", "confidence": 9, "path": "src/session.rs", "line": 34, "category": "error-handling", "summary": "`self.last_seen.lock().unwrap()` will panic if the mutex is poisoned (another task panicked while holding the guard), crashing the async runtime task in production.", "fix": "Replace `.unwrap()` with `.map_err(|e| Error::from(e))?` (or a domain-specific error variant) so poison is propagated as a recoverable error rather than a panic."}]
37abc2ca19729ece: [{"severity": "CRITICAL", "confidence": 10, "path": "src/session.rs", "line": 35, "category": "ownership-lifetimes", "summary": "A std::sync::MutexGuard is held across an .await point: MutexGuard<T> is !Send, so any async runtime that requires Send futures will reject this at compile time, and on single-threaded or work-stealing executors that accept it the lock remains held while the task is suspended, deadlocking every other task that tries to acquire last_seen.", "fix": "Replace std::sync::Mutex with tokio::sync::Mutex (or another async-aware mutex) so the guard is Send and the lock is released at suspension points, or clone/copy the value out of the guard and drop the guard before the .await."}]
c74433e0972798bb: [{"severity": "MEDIUM", "confidence": 8, "path": "src/session.rs", "line": 35, "category": "red-team", "summary": "The new code persists the stale previous value of `last_seen` rather than the current time: `store.persist(*last)` is called before `*last = Instant::now()`, so the durable store always records a timestamp that is one `touch()` call behind reality, regressing from the original behavior of persisting `Instant::now()`.", "fix": "Capture `let now = Instant::now()` before acquiring the lock, call `self.store.persist(now).await?` (dropping the guard first to avoid holding it across await), then re-acquire and write `*last = now`."}]
f6311ff353d398d2: [{"severity":"CRITICAL","confidence":9,"path":"src/session.rs","line":35,"category":"concurrency","summary":"A std::sync::MutexGuard is held across an .await point, blocking the executor thread while the async store.persist() call is suspended and deadlocking any other task that tries to acquire the same lock.","fix":"Release the lock before awaiting by copying the value out first (let last_val = *self.last_seen.lock().unwrap();), then await, then re-acquire the lock to update the timestamp, or switch to tokio::sync::Mutex which is safe to hold across await points."}]
